rationale: Keep: the cited paper is not a generic background match—it directly addresses how elasticity-based cross-price interpretations can be misleading and argues for absolute cross-price effects for brand-switching/profitability, while the citing paper’s core contribution is likewise to correct elasticity-based interpretation by translating to net unit-sales switching effects. Given this close conceptual/methodological overlap on promotion-driven brand switching metrics, this pair plausibly reflects substantive use rather than incidental mention.
